summary:
Iteration 354 — Verified the WhatsApp launcher is now correctly wired into the LIVE route /partner/affiliate-dashboard via AffiliateDashboardHomePage.jsx (CampaignCard sub-component). Code review on the route component (the actual mounted component per App.js line 850) confirms every CampaignCard renders a green (#25D366) Button with label 'WhatsApp', data-testid `whatsapp-share-${idx}`, idx=0..N for groupDeals (line 261) and 1000+i for products (line 271). onClick calls quickShare(c) which builds `https://wa.me/?text=` + URL-encoded(caption || name + savings + product_link) and invokes window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer'). Live click test could not be performed because no approved affiliate exists in DB (all 41 affiliate records are status='pending' — same blocker reported in iter 353). Admin role is redirected to /login by the partner route guard (verified). Per request, code review acceptance is allowed when no live affiliate session is feasible.

Backend regression: /api/admin/force-hydrate still returns the sanitised_descriptions block ({scanned:610, cleaned:0}) — PASS. HOWEVER, marketplace product search /api/marketplace/products/search leaks vendor identity in the `source_url` field on 415/415 items (e.g. 'https://darcity.tz/product-details?Product=...'). This contradicts the 'verified clean' precondition in this iteration's request and the iter-353 zero-leak claim — iter 353 likely scanned only descriptive text fields and missed source_url.

critical.issue: Every product item in the public, unauthenticated search response includes `source_url` = 'https://darcity.tz/product-details?Product=<uuid>' AND `source`='url_import'. 415/415 sampled items leak vendor identity. Affects all queries (q=lanyard, blank q, etc). The vendor-scrub claim is therefore NOT clean for the marketplace search endpoint.

action_items: Strip `source_url` (and any `source`-style vendor fields) from the public response of /api/marketplace/products/search and any related public marketplace listing endpoints. Either drop the field entirely from the public schema, or replace with a non-vendor-identifying internal id. Re-run leak scan against q='', q='lanyard', q='diary' covering the full domain whitelist {'darcity','dar city','sourced from','.tz/product-details'}.

Once an approved affiliate seed account is provisioned (or admin can be allowed to preview the affiliate dashboard), do a live click-and-stub test of the WhatsApp button on /partner/affiliate-dashboard to assert the wa.me URL contains caption + product_link. Pure code review was sufficient this round per the review_request fallback.
critical_code_review_comments: AffiliateDashboardHomePage.jsx — quickShare correctly URL-encodes a multi-line message (caption||name + savings + product_link) and uses window.open with noopener,noreferrer. CampaignCard third button (lines 362-370) is a green #25D366 Button with data-testid `whatsapp-share-${idx}`, label 'WhatsApp', icon MessageCircle. Idx propagation: groupDeals -> i (line 261), products -> 1000+i (line 271). Implementation matches the spec exactly.

Routing: App.js line 850 mounts AffiliateDashboardHomePage at /partner/affiliate-dashboard. The legacy AffiliateProductPromoTable still exists at /partner/affiliate (different route) — both share the WhatsApp share UX with consistent testid format.
Marketplace search leaks source_url (vendor URL) on every public item — see backend_issues.critical above.
Suggestion: also exclude the `source` field ('url_import') as it is a vendor-process leak, even if not the vendor name itself.

success_rate.backend: 50% (force-hydrate sanitised_descriptions PASS; marketplace search vendor scrub FAIL — 415/415 items leak source_url)
success_rate.frontend: 100% via code review (WhatsApp button correctly rendered on the live route per spec; live click test deferred — no approved affiliate)

test_credentials.approved_affiliate: STILL NONE — all 41 records in /api/admin/affiliates have status='pending'. Same blocker as iter 353.

context_for_next_testing_agent: WhatsApp launcher acceptance criterion is satisfied at /partner/affiliate-dashboard via AffiliateDashboardHomePage.jsx (verified by code review of the live-mounted component). The OPEN regression that blocks claiming 'vendor identity stripped from public-facing fields' is in /api/marketplace/products/search returning `source_url: https://darcity.tz/...` and `source: url_import` on every item (415/415 in q='' sample). To reproduce: GET /api/marketplace/products/search?limit=50 (no auth) and inspect any item.source_url. To live-test the WhatsApp button, seed/approve one affiliate account via admin UI (POST /api/admin/affiliates/<id>/approve) and run the existing stub: override window.open before clicking [data-testid^='whatsapp-share-'].
rca_of_the_issue: Vendor source_url leak: the marketplace product documents in MongoDB store the original ingestion URL on `source_url` and the import method on `source`. When /api/marketplace/products/search serialises items it returns the raw document (minus _id), so vendor URLs are exposed publicly. Fix: in the search endpoint's response projection, exclude `source_url`, `source`, and any other ingest-metadata keys from the public payload. A regex/keyword post-filter on description fields alone is insufficient — explicit field whitelisting or projection is required.
